mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets boost corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 5.0% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, supporting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, yielding a negative directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 200.0, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy, depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL is above 200.0, contradicting the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as wealth effects materialize.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when HOUST rises month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real personal savings indicates a healthy labor market funding consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of PAYEMS and RSAFS predicts negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of test periods, invalidating the positive directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized, supply bottlenecks emerge as industrial production fails to keep pace, causing margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, refuting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, weakening labor demand signals an approaching economic slowdown, prompting equity de-rating and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, contradicting the negative directional prediction.
